title: ValueTask should be consumed correctly
description: ValueTask<TResult> provides a value type that wraps a Task<TResult> and the corresponding TResult. It was introduced in .NET Core 2.0 to optimize memory allocation when functions return their results synchronously. Using ValueTask and ValueTask<TResult> in the following ways is discouraged as it could result in a race condition: Calling await multiple times on a ValueTask/ValueTask<TResult>.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects multiple awaits on same ValueTask or accessing .Result after await

checks.message: ValueTask awaited multiple times - this can cause race conditions
ValueTask Result accessed after await - consume only once
ValueTask consumed multiple ways (GetAwaiter and Result) - use only one

logic.triggering_conditions: Multiple awaits on same ValueTask OR .Result after await OR mixed consumption
never_trigger_on: Each call to async method returning new ValueTask
logic.reasoning: ValueTask is designed for single consumption to avoid allocation. Awaiting multiple times or mixing consumption methods can cause race conditions and undefined behavior.

should_trigger: ValueTask<int> vt = GetAsync(); await vt; await vt;
ValueTask<int> vt = GetAsync(); await vt; var r = vt.Result;
ValueTask<int> vt = GetAsync(); vt.GetAwaiter(); var r = vt.Result;
should_not_trigger: ValueTask<int> vt = GetAsync(); Task<int> t = vt.AsTask(); await t; await t;
